Combat sport events in 2023
ONE Fight Night 12: Superlek vs. Khalilov
The poster for ONE Fight Night 12: Superlek vs. Khalilov
PromotionONE Championship
DateJuly 15, 2023 (2023-07-15)
VenueLumpinee Boxing Stadium
CityBangkok, Thailand
Event chronology
ONE Friday Fights 25: Nakrob vs. Phetputhai ONE Fight Night 12: Superlek vs. Khalilov ONE Friday Fights 26: Kulabdam vs. Bohic

ONE Fight Night 12: Superlek vs. Khalilov was a combat sports event produced by ONE Championship that took place on July 15, 2023, at Lumpinee Boxing Stadium in Bangkok, Thailand.

Background

[edit]

A ONE Heavyweight World Championship unification bout between current champion Arjan Bhullar and interim champion Anatoly Malykhin was scheduled to headline the event.[1] The pair were previously scheduled to square off at ONE 161 and then ONE Fight Night 8, but were moved to this event due to a change in broadcast commitments. However, on May 19, 2023, it was announced that the fight was moved to ONE Friday Fights 22.[2]

A rematch for the ONE Featherweight World Championship between current champion Tang Kai and former champion Thanh Le was scheduled to headline the event.[3] However, Tang withdrew from the fight due to a knee injury and the fight was cancelled.[4]

A ONE Light Heavyweight Kickboxing World Championship bout between current champion Roman Kryklia and Françesko Xhaja was expected to headline the event.[5][6] However, Xhaja withdraw from the bout due to injury. As a result, a flyweight muay thai bout between current ONE Flyweight Kickboxing Champion Superlek Kiatmuu9 and Tagir Khalilov promoted to main event status. Khalilov who scheduled to fight Elias Mahmoudi at this event.[7]

A featherweight bout between former ONE Featherweight title challenger and #2 ranked Garry Tonon and #5 ranked Shamil Gasanov served as the co-main event.[8]

A women's atomweight muay thai bout between former WMC (-45 kg) World champion Phetjeeja Lukjaoporongtom and the reigning ISKA Oriental rules World Super Featherweight (−59 kg) champion Lara Fernandez took place at the event.[9]

At the weigh-ins, Superlek Kiatmuu9, Walter Goncalves and Yuya Wakamatsu missed weight. Superlek weighed in at 135.25 lbs, 0.25 pounds over the limit, Goncalves weighed in at 135.75 pounds, 0.75 pounds over the limit, and Wakamatsu weighed in at 135.5 pounds, 0.5 pounds over the limit. All the bouts proceeded at catchweight with each being fined their purse of his purse, which went to their opponents Tagir Khalilov, Banma Duoji and Xie Wei respectively.[10]

Results

[edit]
Main card (Amazon Prime Video)
Weight class Method Round Time Notes
Catchweight (135.25 lb) Muay Thai Superlek Kiatmuu9 def. Tagir Khalilov TKO (punch and elbow) 2 1:42 [a]
Featherweight Garry Tonon def. Shamil Gasanov Submission (kneebar) 2 2:26
Heavyweight Amir Aliakbari def. Dustin Joynson TKO (submission to punches) 1 1:48
Catchweight (135.5 lb) Yuya Wakamatsu def. Xie Wei TKO (punches) 1 2:03 [b]
Featherweight Akbar Abdullaev def. Aaron Cañarte TKO (spinning back kick and punches) 1 0:41
Women's Atomweight Muay Thai Phetjeeja Lukjaoporongtom def. Lara Fernandez TKO (punches) 1 0:26
Catchweight (135.75 lb) Banma Duoji def. Walter Goncalves Decision (unanimous) 3 5:00 [c]
Women's Strawweight Submission Grappling Tammi Musumeci def. Amanda Alequin Decision (unanimous) 1 10:00
Lightweight Kickboxing Bogdan Shumarov def. Constantin Rusu TKO (head kick) 3 1:33
  1. ^ Superlek missed weight (135.25 lb).
  2. ^ Wakamatsu missed weight (135.5 lb).
  3. ^ Goncalves missed weight (135.75 lb).

[11]

Bonus awards

[edit]

The following fighters received $50,000 bonuses.[12]

  • Performance of the Night: Garry Tonon, Amir Aliakbari, Akbar Abdullaev, Phetjeeja Lukjaoporongtom and Bogdan Shumarov
